raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-observation / metacognitive noticing that separates subjective mental events from evidential evaluation",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "automatic thoughts exist as distinct mental events",
    "evidence about what is true exists independently of those thoughts",
    "the speaker is capable of noticing a discrepancy between the two",
    "such noticing has epistemic or therapeutic value"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent; cleanly distinguishes occurrent mental content from an external or evidential standard without contradiction",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the person tends to treat automatic thoughts as automatically true and benefits from explicitly marking the distinction to enable later evidence-checking",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the statement is taken to imply that automatic thoughts are always false or irrelevant, or that 'evidence' is treated as immediately obvious and uncontested"
}
